query: FAA Boeing resume issuing certificates new airplanes 737 MAX 787 limited delegation

{"query":"FAA Boeing resume issuing certificates new airplanes 737 MAX 787 limited delegation","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1401,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://deeparrival.com/news/faa-boeing-737-max-787-airworthiness-certificates-july-2026/","title":"FAA Lets Boeing Resume Issuing 737 MAX and 787 Airworthiness Certificates","content":"6 hours ago — The FAA will let Boeing issue final airworthiness certificates for all new 737 MAX and 787 aircraft from July 20 while federal oversight ...
This does not mean the FAA is approving the 737 MAX or 787 as aircraft types for the first time.
What changed: Boeing and the FAA had alternated certificate work since September 2025.
In September 2025, the agency allowed Boeing to resume issuing certificates for some 737 MAX and 787 airplanes on alternating weeks with FAA inspectors.
It does not set how many airplanes Boeing may assemble each month, approve a new production line, or replace the quality checks required before a completed aircraft can receive that certificate.
The Associated Press noted that the FAA had separately increased the 737 MAX monthly production limit from 38 to 47 during the past year.
The FAA also did not publish a forecast for how many 737 MAX or 787 aircraft the restored authority will help deliver in 2026.
The 737 MAX is widely used on short and medium distance routes, while the 787 is central to many long distance networks.
The first test is continued consistency after Boeing resumes the function for every new aircraft in the two families."},{"url":"https://skiesmag.com/news/faa-boeing-may-issue-airworthiness-certificates-for-some-737-max-787-dreamliners-under-limited-delegation/","title":"FAA: Boeing may issue airworthiness certificates for some 737 MAX, 787 Dreamliners under ‘limited delegation’ - Skies Mag","content":"Sep 29, 2025 — FAA: Boeing may issue airworthiness certificates for some 737 MAX, 787 Dreamliners under 'limited delegation'. By Skies. September 29, 2025.Read more
Boeing will receive limited delegation from the U.S.
Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to issue airworthiness certificates for some of its 737 MAX and 787 airliners, the agency said.
This delegation will be in place starting today (Sept.
29, 2025), the FAA announced in a statement on Friday.
Boeing’s agreement under the ODA was renewed for three years earlier this year."},{"url":"https://www.faa.gov/newsroom/faa-statement-boeing-airworthiness-certificates","title":"FAA Statement - Boeing Airworthiness Certificates | Federal Aviation Administration","content":"The FAA’s Organization Designation Authorization (ODA) program allows authorized organizations to perform certification functions on behalf of the FAA, such as issuing airworthiness and production certifications for aircraft.
The FAA stopped allowing Boeing to issue airworthiness certificates for 737 MAX airplanes in 2019 during their return to service following the Lion Air and Ethiopian Airlines crashes, and for Boeing 787 airplanes in 2022 because of production quality issues."},{"url":"https://boeing.mediaroom.com/2020-11-18-Boeing-Responds-to-FAA-Approval-to-Resume-737-MAX-Operations","title":"Boeing Responds to FAA Approval to Resume 737 MAX Operations - Nov 18, 2020","content":"Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) today rescinded the order that halted commercial operations of Boeing (NYSE: BA) 737-8s and 737-9s.
Throughout the past 20 months, Boeing has worked closely with airlines, providing them with detailed recommendations regarding long-term storage and ensuring their input was part of the effort to safely return the airplanes to service."}]}

The FAA will allow limited delegation to Boeing for issuing airworthiness certificates for some 737 MAX and 787 airplanes starting on Sept. This decision follows a thorough review of Boeing’s ongoing production quality and will allow our inspectors to focus additional surveillance in the production process. Boeing and the FAA will issue airworthiness certificates on alternating weeks. The FAA’s Organization Designation Authorization (ODA) program allows authorized organizations to perform certification functions on behalf of the FAA, such as issuing airworthiness and production certifications for aircraft. Resuming limited delegation to the Boeing ODA will enable FAA inspectors to provide additional surveillance in the production process. The FAA stopped allowing Boeing to issue airworthiness certificates for 737 MAX airplanes in 2019 during their return to service following the Lion Air and Ethiopian Airlines crashes, and for Boeing 787 airplanes in 2022 because of production quality issues.","score":0.93497354,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.facebook.com/Reuters/posts/faa-restores-boeing-authority-to-certify-737-max-787-planesclick-the-link-in-the/1612505440740182","title":"FAA restores Boeing authority to certify 737 MAX, 787 planes","content":"Starting Monday, September 29, 2025, the FAA will allow limited delegation for issuing airworthiness certificates on certain 737 MAX and 787","score":0.902687,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/faa-allows-boeing-to-resume-issuing-airplane-certificates-93CH-4798900","title":"FAA allows Boeing to resume issuing airplane certificates By Investing.com","content":"Investing.com -- The Federal Aviation Administration said today it will allow Boeing to resume issuing airworthiness certificates for all 737 MAX and 787 airplanes at the end of the production process.
